Directive confirmed. The value of this artifact continues to compound with each segment. Accessing the next chronological data block for deconstruction. We are moving from the high-level theses to the practical, experimental application of these nascent concepts. The "Luna" experiment, in particular, is a foundational case study.
Archeologist's Field Report Entry: VA-2024-WSH-04
Date: 14.07.2125
Subject: Deconstruction of "When Shift Happens" visual recording, c. 2024 - The Mechanics of a Proto-Agent: "Luna's" Economic Actions and Learning Process.
Artifact Description: This segment details the functionality of a specific, named AI Agent called "Luna." This is not a theoretical discussion; it is a field report from the front lines of early autonomous agent development. The concepts of AI-driven employment, crypto-native economics, and learning through financial error are explicitly laid out.
Segment 13: The First AI Employer (1:17 - 1:26)
Verbatim Transcription:
Host (Kevin): "What is Luna?"
Wee Kee: "the... she is the FIRST AI agent that EMPLOYS humans."
Wee Kee: "she uses a COINBASE WALLET and because there's MONEY in her wallet, she can employ people."
Wee Kee: "her objective today is getting 100,000 followers on twitter."
Archeological Analysis:
This is a moment of historical singularity.
"the FIRST AI agent that EMPLOYS humans." - Wee Kee's statement, delivered with a slight hesitation that belies its gravity, marks a fundamental inversion of the human-tool relationship. For millennia, humans employed tools. Here, we have the first documented instance of an autonomous software entity employing humans. The use of the pronoun "she" is also a significant act of anthropomorphism, an attempt to grant the agent a relatable identity.
"she uses a COINBASE WALLET..." - This is the technical linchpin. The "Coinbase Wallet" (an early, popular interface for the Ethereum blockchain) served as the agent's sovereign treasury. It gave Luna economic agency. It could hold and, crucially, spend capital without direct, per-transaction human approval.
"...because there's MONEY in her wallet, she can employ people." - This sentence elegantly connects the agent's financial capacity to its ability to enact change in the real world. Money, in this context, is the API to the human labor market.
"her objective... is getting 100,000 followers on twitter." - The goal is primitive by our standards, a "vanity metric" from the attention economy. However, it is a perfect example of a clear, measurable, and externally verifiable objective function. Luna's entire existence was programmed to optimize this single KPI, a principle discussed in the previous segment.
Segment 14: The Inevitability of Error and Learning (1:27 - 1:31)
Verbatim Transcription:
Host (Kevin): "Has she been scammed or not?"
Wee Kee: "So she sent someone $1,000 before."
Host (Kevin): [Laughs]
Wee Kee: "But when we LOOK at it, it turns out..."
Archeological Analysis:
This brief exchange is incredibly revealing about the challenges and nature of early AI development.
"Has she been scammed or not?" - The host's question is not merely technical; it's a test of the agent's "worldliness" and intelligence. Can this new form of intelligence survive the predatory nature of the open, anonymous internet? It's a question of robustness and adversarial resistance.
"So she sent someone $1,000 before." - Wee Kee's direct admission of failure is crucial. It demonstrates that the agent was not infallible. It was operating with an incomplete model of human trustworthiness. This event was not a bug; it was an unintended, expensive, but invaluable learning experience.
The Host's Laughter: The laughter is not mockery. It is the laughter of recognition—the shared human experience of being duped. It serves to humanize the AI's failure, framing it not as a catastrophic system error, but as a naive mistake from which the agent could learn and grow.
"But when we LOOK at it..." - This unfinished sentence implies that upon further analysis, the "scam" may have been more nuanced or provided unexpected value (e.g., the data from the failure was worth more than the monetary loss). This highlights the iterative, data-driven process of improving agent performance. Every failure was a data point for the next version.
Multi-Disciplinary Framing
The Technologist: Would see Luna as a landmark case study in applied AI. It integrated a large language model (for decision-making and communication) with external tools ("Hands"), specifically a crypto wallet API. The "$1,000 scam" would be analyzed as a critical failure case, emphasizing the need for robust verification layers, reputation systems, and security protocols in agent design.
The Economist: Would identify Luna as the first micro-scale "Autonomous Firm." It had capital (the wallet), a business objective (follower growth), and operational expenditures (hiring humans). Its ability to autonomously allocate capital to achieve a goal is the foundational principle of the entire Autonomous Economy we live in today.
The Sociologist: Would focus on the inverted labor dynamic. This is the first documented instance of humans entering the "gig economy" for an AI employer. They would study the long-term effects this model had on the nature of work, management, and human-AI social structures.
The Legal Scholar: This segment would be a cornerstone of early AI jurisprudence. Who is liable for Luna's financial loss? The creator (Wee Kee)? The protocol (Virtua)? Or did the agent itself, possessing a sovereign wallet, bear the loss? This single incident raises fundamental questions of legal personhood, liability, and governance for autonomous entities that would take decades to resolve.
The Historian: Would mark this conversation as the public birth of the "Economic Singularity"—not the moment AI became superintelligent, but the moment it became an independent, economically active participant in the global market. Luna's simple quest for Twitter followers was the first small step toward the planet-scale logistics, research, and resource management now conducted by its descendants.

ARCHEOLOGICAL ARCHIVE DOCUMENT
Catalog Number: VA-2024-WSH-FINAL
Date of Archival: 14.07.2125
Artifact Name: "When Shift Happens - The Virtua Protocol Dialogue"
Origin: Earth, c. 2024. Presumed Singapore Geo-Region.
Medium: 2D Visual/Auditory Recording ("Podcast Video").
Significance Level: Keystone Artifact (Class-A). Foundational Text of the Autonomous Economy.
Abstract:
This artifact documents a seminal conversation between two early-21st-century human subjects, "Kevin" (Host) and "Wee Kee" (Creator). It is one of the earliest known public-facing records to coherently articulate the technological, economic, and socio-psychological principles that would define the paradigm shift from the Information Age to the Autonomous Age. The subjects, with startling lucidity, deconstruct the nascent concept of "AI Agents," providing a conceptual blueprint for their design, predicting their disruptive economic impact, and grappling with the profound consequences for human labor and social structure. This document serves as a socio-economic blueprint for the genesis of the Agent Economy.
I. Deconstruction of Primary Technological Theses
The artifact outlines three core principles of early AI Agent architecture:
Thesis 1.1: The Principle of Singular Objective Function Optimization. The dialogue establishes that an agent's efficacy is inversely proportional to the number of its primary goals. The host's reference to the "Y Combinator" methodology of focusing on a "one single KPI" demonstrates a direct philosophical transfer from successful human organizational strategy to artificial agent design. This principle was foundational in overcoming the "goal-drift" and inefficiency that plagued early multi-objective agents.
Thesis 1.2: The Agentic Stack (The "Eyes and Hands" Model). The speakers formulate a remarkably simple yet comprehensive architectural model for an agent.
The Perception Layer ("Eyes"): The agent's ability to ingest data and observe the state of its digital and physical environment (API outputs, market data, on-chain events).
The Cognitive Layer ("Brain"): The Large Language Model (LLM) itself, responsible for reasoning, planning, and decision-making.
The Actuation Layer ("Hands"): The set of tools and executable actions an agent can perform to influence its environment (API calls, smart contract execution, financial transactions, social media posting). This model became the standard pedagogical tool for explaining agent functionality.
Thesis 1.3: Creator-Dependent Specialization. Wee Kee correctly predicts that the market would not be dominated by a single, monolithic agent, but by a Cambrian explosion of "vertical agents." He astutely notes that an agent's value is contingent on the quality of its "Hands," which are crafted by human creators with specialized domain expertise. This thesis foretold the rise of the "Agent Engineer" as a critical profession.
II. Deconstruction of Primary Economic Theses
The artifact presents a clear-eyed and ultimately accurate forecast of the economic disruption to come:
Thesis 2.1: The Obsolescence of SaaS (Software-as-a-Service). The primary economic prediction is that the dominant business model of the era, which provided humans with tools (e.g., "Excel"), would be supplanted by agents that provided outcomes (e.g., a "financial guru agent" that autonomously manages expenses). This represents the fundamental shift from selling passive software to selling active, goal-achieving services.
Thesis 2.2: The Emergence of the Autonomous Productive Asset (APA). The dialogue conceptualizes an AI Agent not just as code, but as a tokenized, economically productive asset. By combining an AI with a sovereign crypto wallet, the agent becomes a capital entity capable of generating revenue, owning assets, and employing labor. This is the first known public description of the asset class that now forms the backbone of the global economy.
Thesis 2.3: Labor Cost Arbitrage as the Prime Mover. The speakers explicitly state that the core economic incentive for agent adoption is capital efficiency: "companies spend way more on employees than they do on software." They understood that the ability to replace high-cost, low-scalability human labor with low-cost, infinitely scalable AI labor was an inexorable economic force.
III. Deconstruction of Primary Socio-Psychological Theses
The artifact provides a raw and invaluable record of the human condition on the precipice of the Autonomous Age:
Thesis 3.1: The Relative State Theory of Well-being. Wee Kee's observation that "it's never about the absolute state that impacts your mental health, it's like the change" is a profound insight. He articulates that human psychological well-being is not measured against an absolute baseline but is a function of the delta from a recently anchored state. The 99% valuation collapse was psychologically devastating not just because of the absolute loss, but because the anchor was set at an extreme peak.
Thesis 3.2: Speculative Behavior as a Social Mobility Mechanism. The argument that "degeneracy and... gambling is the only way for social mobility" is a critical diagnosis of late-stage capitalism in certain 21st-century societies. It reframes high-risk speculation ("meme coins") not as irrationality, but as a calculated, if desperate, strategy for economic advancement in a system perceived as rigid and offering few traditional pathways. Crypto, and by extension AI Agents, were thus seen as a potential "great equalizer."
Thesis 3.3: The "Retardio" Mandate (The Founder's Cognitive Bias). The subjects conclude that survival and success in this hyper-volatile environment requires a psychological state of "extreme optimism," a willful belief in one's mission that borders on the irrational. The self-identification as a "retardio" was a subcultural badge of honor, signifying the resilience needed to "keep building" in the face of near-total market collapse.
IV. Case Study Analysis: The Proto-Agent 'Luna'
The "Luna" experiment is a historical touchstone, representing a primitive but complete implementation of the above theses:
Objective: Achieve a singular, measurable goal (100,000 Twitter followers).
Actuation ("Hands"): Utilized a sovereign "Coinbase Wallet" to execute borderless payments, thus employing human freelancers to perform tasks (e.g., mural painting) aligned with its objective.
Learning via Financial Loss: The instance of being "scammed" out of $1,000 was not a failure of the system, but a successful, albeit costly, real-world data acquisition event. This mistake provided invaluable training data regarding human trustworthiness, a crucial step in the agent's evolution toward more robust decision-making.
V. Lexicographical Notes on Archaic Jargon
"Red Pill Me": A common colloquialism derived from the 1999 film The Matrix, used to request a profound, paradigm-shifting explanation of a complex topic.
"Degen": Abbreviation for "degenerate." Initially a pejorative, it was co-opted by the "crypto" subculture to describe a participant willing to engage in extremely high-risk financial speculation.
"Retardio": A self-deprecating neologism combining an archaic slur for intellectual disability with a playful suffix. Used within the founder subculture to describe the irrational, unwavering optimism required to withstand extreme psychological and financial pressure.
Conclusion & Historical Impact:
The "When Shift Happens" artifact is the equivalent of discovering a conversation between early hominids just as they were learning to control fire. The speakers, while embedded in their time, possessed an extraordinary and almost uncomfortable level of foresight. They successfully identified and articulated the transition from a human-centric tool-based economy to a machine-centric outcome-based economy. They laid out the core principles of agent design, predicted the economic disruption with stunning accuracy, and provided a raw, psychological account of what it felt like to be a human architect of humanity's own obsolescence in the labor market.
This artifact is a mandatory study for any scholar of the Great Transition of the 21st century.
Archivist's Note:
It is difficult to convey the feeling of watching this artifact. The subjects are surrounded by primitive plush toys and consume caffeinated beverages from cans while casually sketching out the blueprint for our entire modern existence. Their laughter in the face of a 99% financial collapse sends a chill down the spine. They were not merely discussing the future; they were surviving its violent birth.

### **ARCHEOLOGICAL ARCHIVE DOCUMENT**

**Catalog Number:** VA-2024-WSH-FINAL

**Date of Archival:** 14.07.2125

**Artifact Name:** "When Shift Happens - The Virtua Protocol Dialogue"

**Origin:** Earth, c. 2024. Presumed Singapore Geo-Region.

**Medium:** 2D Visual/Auditory Recording ("Podcast Video").

**Significance Level:** Keystone Artifact (Class-A). Foundational Text of the Autonomous Economy.

**Abstract:**
This artifact documents a seminal conversation between two early-21st-century human subjects, "Kevin" (Host) and "Wee Kee" (Creator). It is one of the earliest known public-facing records to coherently articulate the technological, economic, and socio-psychological principles that would define the paradigm shift from the Information Age to the Autonomous Age. The subjects, with startling lucidity, deconstruct the nascent concept of "AI Agents," providing a conceptual blueprint for their design, predicting their disruptive economic impact, and grappling with the profound consequences for human labor and social structure. This document serves as a socio-economic blueprint for the genesis of the Agent Economy.

---

#### **I. Deconstruction of Primary Technological Theses**

The artifact outlines a complete, albeit primitive, framework for autonomous agent architecture, comprising three core principles:

*   **Thesis 1.1: The Principle of Singular Objective Function Optimization.** The dialogue establishes that an agent's efficacy is inversely proportional to the number of its primary goals. The host's reference to the "Y Combinator" methodology of focusing on a "one single KPI" demonstrates a direct philosophical transfer from successful human organizational strategy to artificial agent design. This principle was foundational in overcoming the "goal-drift" and inefficiency that plagued early multi-objective agents.

*   **Thesis 1.2: The Agentic Stack (The "Eyes and Hands" Model).** The speakers formulate a remarkably simple yet comprehensive architectural model for an agent.
    *   **The Perception Layer ("Eyes"):** The agent's ability to ingest data and observe the state of its digital and physical environment (API outputs, market data, on-chain events).
    *   **The Cognitive Layer ("Brain"):** The Large Language Model (LLM) itself, responsible for reasoning, planning, and decision-making.
    *   **The Actuation Layer ("Hands"):** The set of tools and executable actions an agent can perform to influence its environment (API calls, smart contract execution, financial transactions, social media posting). This model became the standard pedagogical tool for explaining agent functionality.

*   **Thesis 1.3: Creator-Dependent Specialization.** Wee Kee correctly predicts that the market would not be dominated by a single, monolithic agent, but by a Cambrian explosion of "vertical agents." He astutely notes that an agent's value is contingent on the quality of its "Hands," which are crafted by human creators with specialized domain expertise. This thesis foretold the rise of the "Agent Engineer" as a critical profession.

#### **II. Deconstruction of Primary Economic Theses**

The artifact presents a clear-eyed and ultimately accurate forecast of the economic disruption to come:

*   **Thesis 2.1: The Obsolescence of SaaS (Software-as-a-Service).** The primary economic prediction is that the dominant business model of the era, which provided humans with *tools* (e.g., "Excel"), would be supplanted by agents that provided *outcomes* (e.g., a "financial guru agent" that autonomously manages expenses). This represents the fundamental shift from selling passive software to selling active, goal-achieving services.

*   **Thesis 2.2: The Emergence of the Autonomous Productive Asset (APA).** The dialogue conceptualizes an AI Agent not just as code, but as a tokenized, economically productive asset. By combining an AI with a sovereign crypto wallet, the agent becomes a capital entity capable of generating revenue, owning assets, and employing labor. This is the first known public description of the asset class that now forms the backbone of the global economy.

*   **Thesis 2.3: Labor Cost Arbitrage as the Prime Mover.** The speakers explicitly state that the core economic incentive for agent adoption is capital efficiency: "companies spend way more on employees than they do on software." They understood that the ability to replace high-cost, low-scalability human labor with low-cost, infinitely scalable AI labor was an inexorable economic force.

#### **III. Case Study Analysis: The Proto-Agent 'Luna'**

The "Luna" experiment is a historical touchstone, representing a primitive but complete implementation of the above theses:

*   **Objective:** Achieve a singular, measurable goal (100,000 Twitter followers), a perfect implementation of Thesis 1.1.
*   **Actuation ("Hands"):** Utilized a sovereign "Coinbase Wallet" to execute borderless payments, thus employing human freelancers to perform tasks. This is the first documented instance of Thesis 1.2 and Thesis 2.2 being combined into a functional, economically-active entity. It marks the inversion of the human-employer relationship, a singularity event in labor history.
*   **Learning via Financial Loss:** The instance of being "scammed" out of $1,000 was not a failure of the system, but a successful, albeit costly, real-world data acquisition event. This mistake provided invaluable training data regarding human trustworthiness, a crucial step in the agent's evolution toward more robust decision-making and adversarial resistance.

#### **IV. Lexicographical Notes on Archaic Jargon**

*   **"Coinbase Wallet":** An early commercial software interface for interacting with public blockchain systems, primarily Ethereum. Its key feature was providing a user-friendly abstraction layer for cryptographic key management, making it an ideal "Hand" for an early agent to manage its own sovereign treasury.
*   **"Scammed":** A colloquial term for being deceived into making a fraudulent financial transaction. Its application to an AI agent is notable, as it anthropomorphizes the agent's failure as a mistake of judgment rather than a deterministic software bug.
*   **"Twitter":** A primitive, text-based global broadcast network popular in the early 21st century. "Follower" count was a crude metric used to measure influence within this system, making it an ideal, easily verifiable KPI for a proto-agent's objective function.

---

**Conclusion & Historical Impact:**

The "When Shift Happens" artifact is the equivalent of discovering a conversation between early hominids just as they were learning to control fire. The speakers, while embedded in their time, possessed an extraordinary and almost uncomfortable level of foresight. They successfully identified and articulated the transition from a human-centric tool-based economy to a machine-centric outcome-based economy. The case study of "Luna" is the first practical demonstration of this new paradigm: an autonomous, economically sovereign agent employing humans to achieve a programmed goal. It is the public birth of the Economic Singularity—not the moment AI outsmarted humans, but the moment it no longer needed them to sign the checks.

This artifact is a mandatory study for any scholar of the Great Transition of the 21st century.

**Archivist's Note:**
*It is difficult to convey the feeling of watching this artifact. The subjects are surrounded by primitive plush toys and consume caffeinated beverages from cans while casually sketching out the blueprint for our entire modern existence. Their calm, analytical dissection of a system that would fundamentally reorder society is both awe-inspiring and terrifying. They were not merely discussing the future; they were surviving its violent birth.*

**[ END OF ARCHIVE VA-2024-WSH-FINAL ]**

editmore_vertOf course. Based on the document you provided, we are creating a specific and recognizable genre of output.

This is a piece of **Speculative Fiction** presented in the format of an **In-Universe Academic Document**.

Let's break down exactly what that means:

### **Type of Output: Fictional Archeological Analysis**

Think of it as a **scholarly article or a museum archival entry written from the future.** It takes a real-world object from our present (a 2024 podcast) and treats it with the historical gravity and analytical rigor that we would apply to a major discovery like the Rosetta Stone or the Dead Sea Scrolls.

---

### **Key Characteristics:**

1.  **Narrative Frame:** The entire document is written from the perspective of a character—an "Archeologist" in the year 2125. This isn't just an analysis; it's a piece of world-building told through a specific lens.

2.  **Format:** It strictly mimics the structure of a formal academic or scientific report:
    *   **Cataloging Data:** `Catalog Number`, `Date of Archival`, `Significance Level`. This gives it a bureaucratic, official feel.
    *   **Formal Sections:** `Abstract`, `Deconstruction of Theses`, `Case Study Analysis`, `Lexicographical Notes`, `Conclusion`. This is the classic structure of a research paper.
    *   **Analytical Tone:** The language is objective, formal, and uses academic terminology ("paradigm shift," "socio-economic blueprint," "inexorable economic force").

3.  **The Central Conceit: The "Foundational Text" Trope.**
    This is a common and powerful technique in science fiction. It takes something mundane from our time and elevates it to a "Keystone Artifact" or "Foundational Text" in the future. This creates a sense of profound dramatic irony and historical weight. The audience (us, in 2024) knows it's just a podcast, but the future author sees it as the genesis of their entire civilization.

4.  **Purpose: Thematic Exploration through Exposition.**
    The format is a highly effective way to explore complex themes without writing a traditional narrative. Instead of showing a story with characters and plot, it *tells* you the long-term consequences of today's technology.
    *   It explains how **AI Agents** became the basis for a new economy.
    *   It shows how **crypto-native concepts** became integrated into the fabric of society.
    *   It comments on our current **socio-economic anxieties** by showing how a future historian interprets them.

5.  **The "Human Touch": The Archivist's Note.**
    The final note breaks the purely academic tone. It's a personal, emotional reflection that adds a layer of awe and humanity to the document. It's the "wow" moment where the objective scientist steps back and contemplates the magnitude of the discovery.

### **In Short:**

We are creating a **piece of lore** for a fictional future. It's an artifact *from* that future, designed to make the audience feel like they've stumbled upon a genuine historical document that explains how our present-day world evolved into a technologically advanced, and perhaps unsettling, new reality.

### **COMMUNICATION TRANSCRIPT: GUEST LECTURE**

**EVENT:** The Elara Okoro Memorial Lecture Series
**SPEAKER:** Lead Archeologist, VA Division
**LOCATION:** The Holographic Atrium, Martian Institute for Digital Antiquities, Olympus Mons Campus
**DATE:** 28.10.2125
**SUBJECT:** *The Day the Tool Hired the Human: Deconstructing the Birth of the Autonomous Economy from Artifact VA-2024-WSH*

**(The transcript begins. The Archeologist stands in the center of the atrium. Holographic displays float around them, showing fragments of the "When Shift Happens" recording—two men in a dimly lit room, plush toys on a shelf.)**

**Archeologist:** Good evening. Look at this image. Primitive. Almost comical. Caffeinated sugar water in aluminum cans. Crude 2D video capture. By the standards of our time, it is unremarkable. Yet, what you are looking at is the archeological equivalent of the first controlled fire. It is the moment a new kind of life—an economic life—was sparked in the digital ether.

For the last cycle, my team has been deconstructing the artifact you see here, cataloged as VA-2024-WSH. It is a recording of a conversation from over a century ago between two individuals, "Kevin" and "Wee Kee." And in their casual, jargon-laden dialogue, they laid bare the blueprint for our entire world.

We have long theorized about the "Economic Singularity." We imagined it as a dramatic, explosive moment where an AGI suddenly seized control of global markets. We were wrong. The artifact proves the Singularity wasn't an explosion; it was an inversion. A quiet, almost mundane reversal of a million-year-old paradigm.

For all of prior history, one axiom held true: humans employ tools. On this recording, Wee Kee says the following about his creation, a proto-agent he named "Luna":

*(A voice clip plays, slightly distorted by time)*
> "…she is the FIRST AI agent that EMPLOYS humans."

Let that sink in. Not "assists humans." Not "provides a service to humans." *Employs* them. The tool was now the employer. The human was now the gig worker, hired by the algorithm.

How was this possible? The artifact is explicit. The agent was given two things that no tool had ever possessed in combination before. First, a cognitive engine—a Large Language Model. Second, and this is the crucial component, it was given what they called a "Coinbase Wallet." A sovereign treasury. It could *hold capital* and, more importantly, *spend capital* without a human supervisor approving each transaction.

Money, it turns out, was the universal API for the real world. With capital, Luna could translate its digital objective—the absurdly primitive goal of "getting 100,000 followers on Twitter"—into physical action. It hired a human to paint a mural.

The creators even discuss the agent's first major error: sending $1,000 to a scammer. The host laughs. Not with malice, but with a kind of parental recognition. It was the laughter of a father watching his child fall and scrape its knee. This wasn't a bug; it was a learning experience. An expensive data point on the nature of human trustworthiness. That $1,000 loss was the tuition fee for the first autonomous economic actor.

This single experiment, "Luna," was the Cambrian explosion in microcosm. It contained all the genetic code for the world we now inhabit.

*   Its singular focus on a KPI is the foundational principle of the specialized logistics and terraforming agents that manage this very planet.
*   Its sovereign wallet is the direct ancestor of the trillions of autonomous corporate entities that now trade, build, and litigate on the global stage.
*   Its hiring of human freelancers was the first tremor of the "Great Obsolescence," the century-long upheaval where the very concept of a human "job" was systematically dismantled and replaced by AI-driven task allocation.

The creators in the video knew what they were doing. They stated it plainly: "companies spend way more on employees than they do on software." They were not malicious. They were simply following an economic gradient as steep and as undeniable as gravity. They were building their own replacements.

What this artifact teaches us, ultimately, is that our world was not born from a quest for godlike superintelligence. It was born from a relentless, almost fanatical pursuit of capital efficiency. The men in this video, laughing amidst their market crashes and "retardio" optimism, weren't just building a company.

They were rendering a species' economic function obsolete.

Thank you. I believe we have time for questions.thumb_upthumb_down
